{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the optimization problem\n\\[\n\\begin{aligned}\n& \\min_{x\\in\\mathbf{R}^n} f(x) \\\\\n\\text{subject to}\\quad & \\begin{cases}\n c_i(x)=0, & i\\in\\mathcal{E}, \\\\\n c_i(x)\\ge 0, & i\\in\\mathcal{I}.\n\\end{cases}\n\\end{aligned}\n\\tag{12.1}\n\\]\nShow that the feasible set \\(\\Omega\\) is convex if \\(c_i\\), for \\(i\\in\\mathcal{E}\\), are linear functions and \\(-c_i\\), for \\(i\\in\\mathcal{I}\\), are convex functions."}
